title: Change entire page to burgundy and white color scheme and add Google Maps location

requirements.text: Change the entire website color scheme to burgundy (guindo) and white, applying burgundy as the primary accent color across all sections including hero section, about section, enrollment form, footer, and any other page elements. Update all CSS variables and Tailwind configurations to use burgundy and white consistently throughout the application.

quotes: Quiero que cambies toda la página de color a guind CON BLANCO

acceptanceCriteria: All page sections use burgundy and white color scheme
Hero section background and overlays use burgundy/white
About section cards and backgrounds use burgundy/white
All text colors maintain proper contrast for readability

requirements.text: Add an interactive map section displaying the location of COBAD 35 de Gómez Farías using the Google Maps embed with the URL https://maps.app.goo.gl/7vmjBD4RjaFTk1gY8. The map should be embedded as an iframe and positioned prominently on the page so visitors can easily see where the school is located.

quotes: quiero que agregues esta dirección que se vea un mapa para que vean dónde está situado el cobad 35 de Gómez farías https://maps.app.goo.gl/7vmjBD4RjaFTk1gY8

acceptanceCriteria: Map section displays Google Maps embed showing COBAD 35 de Gómez Farías location
Map uses the provided Google Maps URL: https://maps.app.goo.gl/7vmjBD4RjaFTk1gY8
Map is responsive and displays properly on mobile and desktop devices
Map section includes a heading or label indicating it shows the school location
Map section follows the burgundy and white color scheme for surrounding elements

constraints: Maintain existing functionality for enrollment and questions forms
Preserve WhatsApp and Instagram contact button functionality
Keep all existing form validation and backend submission logic intact
Do not modify immutable frontend paths (useInternetIdentity, useActor, main.tsx, components/ui)
nonGoals: Adding new form fields or changing form data structure
Modifying backend Motoko code or data models
Changing the success message content or behavior
Adding new social media integrations beyond existing WhatsApp and Instagram
